title: How to Structure the Core 30
content_md:
# How to Structure the Core 30

- Code: e64641a7
- Source: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=e64641a74ba146208b675d47f569c09b
- Video: https://www.loom.com/share/6657820a871c4ac0844b0fd7e16c14f8?sid=16237967-f17c-4c63-b97f-ed71cc6cbc6d?hide_owner=true&hide_share=true&hide_title=true&hideEmbedTopBar=true
- Duration: 5m 22s
- Resources:
  - [Core 30 Website Structure](None)
  - [Done-For-You SEO Services](https://icecreamtruck.shop/collections/our-services)

## Description

_(no description)_

transcript.text: OK, so this is how we structure core 30 local website.
So I'll start with the home page or GVP landing page.
If it's a single location company,
meaning there's one GVP, one location,
the home page will almost always be the GVP landing page.
If it's multi-location, then there
would be separate landing pages for each.
So usually home page, sometimes GVP landing page.
Now the target keyword for the GVP landing page
is going to be the GVP primary category city name,
Plummer Houston, right?
Plummer Chicago, Plummer Fort Wayne, Indiana, OK?
Now that's your target keyword.
So you're going to optimize around that keyword.
You're going to use the exact match keyword like that
in the title tag and in the H1.
Now your title tag in H1 will include exact match
phrase for that target keyword.
So your title in H1 will include Plummer Chicago, Plummer
Houston, but you can have more words in there, right?
Find out why we're the best Plummer in Chicago, 2025
or whatever, right?
OK, now the Core 30 I have in blue here.
These are all of our secondary category pages
and our service pages.
Now the GVP doesn't actually call them secondary categories.
You just add up to 10.
So for this example, we have three secondary categories,
so four categories total, and we have 11 services.
You should have more than 11 services,
but this is just showing what to do.
So we have one URL for each GVP category.
So on the home page, we're going to have subheadings
for each GVP category, 5,200 words about that category,
and in that 50 to 100 word block,
we're going to link to the URL that we have dedicated
to that category.
We'll do that here, here, and here.
Now, sometimes there are super important critical services
that the client really, really wants to rank for.
We usually don't do more than two or three of these
that we'll actually link to from the home page.
So for a plumber, this might be water heater replacement,
and it might be main drain line replacement.
Both of those are very high profit margin jobs
that plumbers really, really want.
So we might track those, especially
for our local rank tracking, and we'll link to those services
directly from our home page.
Same structure, where we're going
to have the GVP service, city name
is going to be the target keyword.
We'll have a subheading that includes that,
block of text below that subheading
that links to this long form article about it.
Okay, all of these blue boxes,
the target keyword is going to be GVP secondary category
or GVP service with the city name, right?
Water heater replacement, Houston.
Emergency plumber, Houston, whatever it happens to be.
Then we have a locations page,
just as a default normal locations page.
Earlier I talked about the three phases
of an SEO engagement.
If you need more topical relevance,
if you finish the core 30
and your rank map doesn't look very good yet,
then you need more topical relevance.
That is what is in green here.
Now these are providing topical relevance
for GVP service three.
If they were providing topical relevance
for the primary category,
they'd be linking up to the home page, okay?
These are just here to make it easier to link to.
So these supporting articles are going to be based
on the people also ask questions with city name.
So if one of the people also ask questions
is how much money does a plumber make,
then you're going to say how much plumber,
how much money does a plumber make in Houston, okay?
We're always gonna add the city name here.
And if you are searching for plumber Houston,
you don't see a people also ask section,
take out the word Houston, add the word what,
add the word how, then the people also ask should come up.
If your rank map looks pretty good,
you're moving past topical relevance
into building phase three,
into building geographical relevance.
Now you need to start building location pages,
which I have shown here in magenta.
Sure, we'll go with magenta.
So for these, we're going to have the GVP primary category
or whatever keyword we're tracking
and the geographic landmark.
So this might be plumber River Oaks,
River Oaks being a neighborhood in Houston.
This might be plumber I-10 and 6-10 interchange,
that being a major interchange in the city of Houston.
Whatever geographic landmark you're trying to turn green,
that's going to be your target keyword.
Instead of Houston, it's gonna be the geographic landmark.
Hopefully this makes sense for how we structure
this local site for the core 30,
for the additional topical relevance,
for the additional geographic relevance.
If you have a multi-location site,
then this GVP landing page, you'll have multiple ones.
Everything else that exists below here,
you're going to build out exactly like this structure.
So yes, if you have three GVPs,
your core 30 suddenly becomes a core 90.
You're going to build specific pages for every category,
every service on each of the three GVPs.
Now, for multi-location site,
it's even more important than normal
to make sure you're watching your local rank map.
There's a really good chance
that you're not gonna finish the core 30
for your third location
before your topical relevance looks really good,
and you just need to start trying
to build geographic relevance
for your second, for your third location.

summary: This lesson explains how to structure a local website for SEO using the Core 30 method. It involves creating category and service pages with specific target keywords, optimizing title tags and H1 headings, and building location-specific content.
key_points: Optimize home page or GVP landing page with exact match keywords
Create subheadings and 5,200-word blocks for each secondary category
Link to detailed service pages from the home page
Use people also ask questions to create supporting articles
Build location-specific content using geographic landmarks
